instrument: FX majors — creator prefers AUDUSD & GBPUSD; backtest across pairs to find best fit
session: London (creator trades 06:00–10:00 London time); backtest every session to see which works best
direction: With-trend only — longs in an uptrend, shorts in a downtrend. Trend = market structure (HH/HL = up, LH/LL = down); when structure breaks, flip bias to the opposite trend.
indicators: None required — pure price action (candlestick pattern + market-structure trend read). An MA could stand in for the trend filter when automating.
entry_trigger: Three-line strike continuation. Uptrend: 3 consecutive bullish candles, then ONE bearish candle that engulfs/retraces that move -> enter LONG at the CLOSE of that engulfing candle. Downtrend mirror: 3 bearish candles + 1 bullish engulfing candle -> SHORT at its close. The engulfing candle is the fake-out retracement; entry is in the trend direction. Always wait for candle close; never enter early.
filters: (1) Trade only with the prevailing trend (skip three-line strikes against it). (2) Skip the setup if the engulfing candle's range > 10 pips — keeps the stop <= 10 pips and the R:R intact.
stop: Fixed 10 pips, placed beyond the engulfing/strike extreme.
targets: Fixed 20 pips (2:1 R:R). No partials, no trail.
summary: With-trend candlestick continuation. A 'three-line strike' (3 candles with the trend + 1 engulfing pullback candle) marks the end of a retracement; enter at that candle's close in the trend direction with a 10-pip stop and 20-pip target. Creator claims ~70-75% WR on AUDUSD/GBPUSD during London. Novel vs the current book (no three-line-strike detector exists); fully mechanical and directly backtestable on FX 5m. PASS bar: PF >= 1.3, avg-R > 0, ~100+ trades, beats a with-trend control, corr < 0.60 to live book.
